entry.title: SAP Developer News, November 7th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/11/07/sap-developer-news-november-7th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

CAP October 2024 release

  • Release notes https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/oct24

New Adaptation Editor & Hands-on SAP Dev live stream series

  • Adaptation Editor: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/introducing-the-new-adaptation-editor-experience-for-sapui5-adaptation/ba-p/13923903
  • Hands-on SAP Dev – Back to Basics – Web Development: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sabpKj-Hf3c

Creating remote data in HDI

  • hdbfabricvirtualtable in HDI: https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogarticleprintpage/blog-id/technology-blog-sap/article-id/176359
  • Step by Step guide to creating remote data in HDI: https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs/blogarticleprintpage/blog-id/technology-blog-sap/article-id/176359
  • Advanced Strategies for Intelligent Data Applications on SAP HANA Cloud from Devtoberfest: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/advanced-strategies-for-intelligent-data-applications-on-sap-hana-cloud/ev-p/13862880

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 CAP October 2024 release

2:25 New Adaptation Editor & Hands-on SAP Dev live stream series

3:38 Creating remote data in HDI

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for November 7th, 2024.

[DJ] Hello, I'm just on my way to Flexso Tech Day 2024 Tomorrow, I'm in my hotel room in Brussels here in Belgium, but I've just got time to tell you about the October 2024 release of CAP. As usual, there's a great set of new features, including the first one about CAPire. you're looking at configuration properties, you can now hover over certain elements and it will show you exactly how and where to make those configurations. In the CDS language and compiler area there's a new type, a new core type, CDS.map. Now this is represented in OData V4 as an empty open complex type and currently is only supported by CAP Java and of course with OData V4 as well. In the Node.js runtime there's an extension to allow the customization of the request limits when making dollar batch requests and in Java there are plenty of changes some important ones one relating to the dependency removal for a deprecated multi-tenancy library there's also some authorization hardening relating to tenant lifecycle endpoints and of course as I've already just mentioned there's new basic support for the new CDS map type which is in beta. There are also in the Java language runtime enhancements for CDS SQL and also enhancements for OData v4. There is also finally in the Java section some new tool support for ams talking of tools there's some general facilities in vs code with the extension for renaming cds files so that you don't accidentally rename files and clobber yourself because you're referencing those files in a using statement what's not to like. Anyway, read all about these October 2024 changes in Capire and I'll see you online.

[Nico] Hi everyone and welcome to the SAP Developer News. I'm here at the Flexso Tech Day 2024 in Belgium together with my good friend DJ and I do have a few Fiori UI5 and web development related updates for you. First of all, the new adaptation editor for the SAP Business Application Studio was just released. It's part of the SAP Fiori tools for the Business Application Studio and comes with some handy new updates and a nice updated experience. Definitely check it out if you are working with adaptation projects. And second of all, I have just started a new Livestream series as part of Hands-on SAP Dev. It's about back to basics for web development. So if you're a web developer or just starting out, we are going back to the basics, covering the absolute fundamentals, and then eventually we're gonna work our way up to more advanced topics, such as UI5, UI elements, and all that good stuff. And also the SAP Cloud Application Program Model. So we're covering both front-end and back-end. Definitely check it out, and I hope to see you there. Bye.

[Witalij] Are you working with the HANA deployment infrastructure? The HDB Fabric Virtual Table plugin in HDI, introduced earlier this year, enables you to add a system-owned replica to a virtual table from a design time fabric virtual table. If you're unsure what it means, please check the Devtoberfest session, Advanced Strategies for Intelligent Data Applications on SAP HANA Cloud, where product manager, Volker Sago, explained and demoed it. Now, his recently published blog post, step-by-step guide for creating remote data in HDI provides a comprehensive walkthrough for setting up remote data in an HDI container on SAP HANA Cloud. It begins with the preparation phase, which includes obtaining and importing the necessary certificates for secure connections. The next step involves defining the remote source using the appropriate configurations and credentials. The guide also covers creating a custom user provided service or CUPS to grant the necessary rights to HDI containers. Additionally, it explains how to define virtual tables within the HDI container. This detailed guide is essential for technical users looking to integrate remote data sources into the SAP HANA Cloud environment efficiently.

entry.title: PMU-20173 - Setting Up Single Sender for Email Notifications creates RMK problem
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm writing about PMU-20173. This new feature solves one problem, but creates a bigger problem. This will solve the problem of performance emails coming from an unrecognizable email address and going to spam or being reported as phishing. This can still be an unmonitored email box because SF PMGM will place the responses into the PMGM module. 

However, since the same single email address is now required to be used in RMK, all automated external recruiting emails will now come from the same address used for performance management. This is a MAJOR problem. Currently, our automated RMK emails are coming from an email box monitored by Talent Acquisition. PMU-20173 now forces us to change those RMK emails to come from another box intended for PMGM, time sheet notifications, PTO notifications, etc.

So, lets look further at solutions:

1. Use an unmonitored email for RMK which will also be used for PMGM and all other notifications. The downside to this is a decrease in the ease of communications for job applicants. A question I have for SAP is, will the job applicant replying to the unmonitored box, get an email kickback saying the email is not valid?

2.  Use a monitored email for PMGM and RMK. The people monitoring that inbox will receive correspondence from candidates, but they would also get highly sensitive performance review information. This is not viable. 

Another question for SAP: We currently have this enabled in prod but it doesn't impact RMK and PMGM notifications. What happens to our current RMK notifications, PMGM notifications, and other notifications if we disable the single sender after 2H2024

Thank you

I get this error:

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant.cloud.sap/api/v1/scim2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"invalidValue","detail":"Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema. content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

What am I doing wrong?

entry.title: Your Sherlock Holmes – Why Product-Specification Configuration Errors Out in the Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Introduction

In my previous blog Your Sherlock Holmes - Why a Fiori App Cannot be Found in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?, I discussed the tight binding of business catalogs and business roles and its direct impact to user’s access to the system. While writing a new blog From A to Z: Setup a Starter System of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition [2024 Edition], I experienced another challenge in this regard: I was denied doing business process configuration. Here is the story to share with you.

 

Scenario

Recently I set up two SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems, one is the Starter System and another one is the 3 System Landscape (3SL) system. It went smoothly for all scope activation, organization structure setup, and the final milestone confirmation.  However, when I start doing the Product-Specific Configuration, the system takes me to the Customizing Tenant 100 without any problem, but I got the below error message. By the way, I do have the BPC_EXPERT user role assigned.

Error: No authorization to start the UI for transaction S_ER1_83000079

This not just happened to a few business processes, but all of them. What went wrong?

 Configure Your Business Processes in the Central Business Configuration Tenant

 

Investigation

Naturally, I investigated the user authorization in the Customizing Tenant 100. The ZBR_BPC_EXPERT user role was copied from SAP business role template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT, and it was assigned to me.

Business Role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT

There is one area caught my attention: there are only 109 Business Catalogs in this business role.  In comparison, other systems have 220+ business catalogs for this BPC_EXPERT role. Why do I have only a half of business catalogs?  I can manually do a comparison and add them in. But it is tedious.

One of my colleagues asked me a critical question at the time: when did you create your ZBR_BPC_EXPERT business role?  From my vague memory, I remembered I did the business role creation after the CBC milestone was confirmed. Is my memory correct?

The business role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT change log (by click on Display Changes button in the above figure) shows the role was created on October 22, 2024 at 18:20:04.

 Change Log of the Business Role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT

To find out when the business catalogs became available, I need to check the CBC Staging transport.  By opening the Fiori app Export Customizing Transport, it lists three transports. The oldest one G6IK900007 is for initial configuration. That is the one I am looking for.

 Fiori app Export Customizing Transport

Opening the transport G6IK900007, then click on Display CBC Staging button, we can see exactly when this transport content was created.

Note: on this screen, it shows Changed on 11/05/2204, because I did a Check on this transport. So, this time stamp can no longer provide the creation date information I am looking for.  That prompts me to go deeper to find out the original transport creation date and time by clicking the Display CBC Staging button.

 Details of Transport G6IK900007

The CBC Staging screen displays the detailed time information I am looking for.  This staging transport was created on 10/23/2024 at 15:30:25, and completed (another Changed) on 10/23/2024, at 20:56:14.

Note: In my blog From A to Z: Setup a Starter System of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition [2024 Edition], I mentioned it took more than six hours for the milestone confirmation to complete. The above timestamp indicates the transport creation took 5 hours 26 minutes.

 BC Staging Information

Now I found the cause of the problem: I created the business role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT one day earlier than the CBC Staging transport was created. In other words, not all the needed business catalogs were available at that time. That’s why ZBR_BPC_EXPERT only contains 109 available business catalogs, not 200+.

Here is a quick explanation on what Milestone Confirmation (activity Confirm Scope and Organizational Structure Phase is Completed) does on the CBC tenant. After you activate the scopes, set up the organization structure, you confirm the milestone. At this step, it transfers all the necessary information based on your configuration from the CBC to the S/4 Customizing Tenant 100, all needed business catalogs and business role templates become visible. Subsequently, the Customizing Tenant 100 puts all this configuration information, including business catalogs and business role templates into the BP staging transport so that they can be transported to the Test Tenant and Production Tenant subsequently. On a new Customizing Tenant 100 before this BC staging transport is created due to milestone confirmation, there are only a limited number of business catalogs and business role templates. 

In our case, the business role template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT was available but only contains 109 business catalogs. The number of business catalogs is increased after the milestone is confirmed.  When I created a user role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT ahead of time, it only contains the available 109 business catalogs.  This is the root cause of my authorization error when doing work process configurations.

 

Solution

Now we need to update the business role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT.  We do this by using the Fiori App Business Role Templates. Within this app, we select Different From Business Roles in the filter Show Business Role Templates. Then hit Go button. Now we see there are three role templates different from roles copied from them, including template SAP_BR_PBC_EXPERT.

 Find Out Business Role Templates different from Business Roles

By clicking on the arrow at the end of row SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT, we find there are 223 business catalogs. Hooray!

To fix the problem, click on the Business Roles tab.  Here you can see I have three business roles all copied from the same template SAP_BR_PBC_EXPERT, but only one role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT is different from the template. Note: the other two roles were created later after CBC milestone was confirmed.

After selecting the role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT and click on the Compare -> Business Catalogs button, a comparison screen shows up. Most probably you need to scroll down the list to expose those business catalogs not included in the role yet.

 Compare Business Role ZBR_BPC_EXPERT with Its Template

After finding those missing business catalogs, you can individually select those business catalogs you need and then hit the Save button, or just hit the Apply All button to include all available business catalogs.  In my case, I need all of them.

 Add More Business Catalogs to a Business Role

 

Conclusion

From above investigation we learned that not only assigning a business role to a user is important, but “when” the business role was created is also important.  If we create a business role too early before all business catalogs are available, we might not get sufficient authorizations.  However, this problem can be resolved by synchronizing your business roles with their relevant templates.

It also worth pointing out that after a major upgrade, the business catalogs within a business role template might be changed. You need to update the business roles with the latest templates accordingly.

 

References

  • Blog: From A to Z: Setup a Starter System of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition [2024 Edition]
  • Blog: Your Sherlock Holmes - Why a Fiori App Cannot be Found in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

entry.title: Re: Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Two
entry.content:
@tylerrose 

Thank you for the comments - I think for the next blog (Part #5) I'll look at why my stories look like they do. For me design is a science not art so my dashboards look like the do for good reason. I'm a practitioner of IBCS so each element is considered, each chart chosen to convey a message etc.

Have a look for IBCS on Youtube - some great tips on design, layout and the WHY stories look like they do.

I will document some design tips in my next blog and we'll use this story (below) as the basis for the design.

Look out for the next installment before the end of the year.

Daniel
